overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Washington state pharmacists can honor out-of-state valid prescriptions, but strict electronic prescribing mandates apply.
overrides.schedule_ii_transfer_text: CII prescriptions must be transmitted electronically per ESB 5380.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: ESB 5380 requires transmission of virtually all prescriptions electronically, including out-of-state.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Typically requires an established practitioner-patient relationship and compliance with both federal and WA telehealth standards.
overrides.special_restrictions: PMP query mandates are rigorously enforced before dispensing complex scheduling.

access_facts.telehealth_services_available: Widespread but restricted initially for controlled substances.
access_facts.notes: State geography makes telehealth popular, but strict initial evaluation rules apply for Schedule II.

rules.rule_text: All prescriptions must be transmitted to the pharmacy electronically, subject to very narrow waivers.

restrictions.description: Paper scripts are not accepted from out-of-state providers without statutory exceptions.
restrictions.workaround: Have the prescriber send the prescription via EPCS system.

segment_data.key_friction: Out of state providers failing to transmit via valid Washington EPCS networks.

items.guidance_text: Confirm that your provider uses an electronic prescribing network that interoperates with Washington pharmacies.

claims.claim_text: Washington requires electronic transmission for all controlled substances.
Mandatory check of the Prescription Review program.

overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Pharmacists may fill out-of-state valid prescriptions subject to verification criteria.
overrides.schedule_ii_transfer_text: Required to be sent electronically directly from the practitioner.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: Oregon adheres to widespread electronics mandates for controlled schedules.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Controlled substances typically require an initial evaluation or specific telemedicine exceptions.
overrides.special_restrictions: Documentation of provider credentials across borders may be required.

access_facts.telehealth_services_available: Limited initial controlled substance prescribing via telehealth.
access_facts.notes: Telehealth coverage is high but strict regulations surrounding Ryan Haight exceptions persist.

rules.rule_text: Schedule II prescriptions must be e-prescribed and cannot be refilled.

restrictions.description: Paper prescriptions for controlled substances are largely blocked.

segment_data.key_friction: Out-of-state prescribers must verify electronic linkage.

items.guidance_text: If prescribed out-of-state, ensure the provider has transmitted the e-rx to your specific Oregon pharmacy accurately.

claims.claim_text: Oregon pharmacists must integrate with the central PDMP.

overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Pharmacists may fill controlled substances from contiguous and non-contiguous states subject to tight regulatory checks in the PMP.
overrides.schedule_ii_transfer_text: CII prescriptions must be transmitted electronically.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: Electronic prescribing is mandated by Assembly Bill 310.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Rules align with federal DEA laws regarding initial in-person evaluations.
overrides.special_restrictions: Reviewing standard patient history inside the PMP is explicitly required.

access_facts.telehealth_services_available: Limited initial controlled substance prescribing via telehealth.
access_facts.notes: E-prescribing strictness for controlled substances complicates crossing dispensing jurisdictions.

rules.rule_text: Assembly Bill 310 mandates the electronic transmission of all controlled substances.

restrictions.description: Nevada generally mandates electronic prescribing for all controlled substances.

segment_data.key_friction: Verification lag for out-of-state paper prescriptions.

items.guidance_text: Request electronic prescriptions directly to a Nevada pharmacy before traveling.

claims.claim_text: Nevada enforces electronic prescribing via AB 310.

overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Prescriptions from out-of-state prescribers are valid if they conform to Arizona's e-prescribing rules.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: HB 2075 mandates electronic prescribing for all Schedule II controlled substances.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Prescribing controlled substances typically requires a face-to-face encounter under federal regulations.

access_facts.notes: E-rx mandate creates the most friction for out-of-state handwritten prescriptions.

rules.rule_text: HB 2075 mandates the electronic transmission of Schedule II substances.

restrictions.description: Paper prescriptions are not accepted for Schedule II controlled substances.

segment_data.key_friction: Strict e-prescribing and monitoring programs restrict casual out of state prescription deposits.

items.guidance_text: Be prepared to encounter delays if your prescriber utilizes paper scripts, which are heavily restricted in AZ.

claims.claim_text: AZ CSPMP checks are mandatory prior to dispensing.

overrides.out_of_state_controlled_text: Pharmacists can dispense out-of-state valid prescriptions, but strict checks in PDMP are customary.
overrides.schedule_iii_v_transfer_text: Permitted with up to 5 refills over 6 months.
overrides.e_rx_scope_text: Colorado law mandates electronic prescribing for all Schedule II-V controlled substances.
overrides.telehealth_controlled_text: Controlled substances prescribed via telehealth are restricted, tracking DEA guidelines.
overrides.special_restrictions: Use of specialized electronic prescribing software is required.

access_facts.telehealth_services_available: Limited initial controlled substance prescribing via telehealth.
access_facts.notes: Remote tracking restricts fast cross-state dispensation.

rules.rule_text: Pharmacists are generally expected to use professional judgment and query the PDMP system prior to dispensing.

restrictions.description: Electronic prescribing is universally required for Schedule II-V.

segment_data.key_friction: PDMP tracking cross-state can cause prescription verification delays.

items.guidance_text: Out of state prescribers should utilize e-prescribing platforms that integrate securely with Colorado pharmacies.

claims.claim_text: Colorado EPCS mandate requires virtual transmission.
